info.name: Auth – Unlock (POST /api/v1/auth/request-unlock + POST /api/v1/auth/confirm-unlock)
info.description:
E2E tests for POST /api/v1/auth/request-unlock and POST /api/v1/auth/confirm-unlock.

Folder execution order (orchestrated by make e2e-unlock):
  1. happy-path        — setup (register, verify-email, trigger time-lock, request-unlock + Gmail OTP fetch)
                          then: wrong OTP→422, correct OTP→200, login-after-unlock→200, replay→422, no-token→422
  2. validation        — all 422/413 input-error cases for both /request-unlock and /confirm-unlock
  3. anti-enumeration  — not-locked→202, unknown→202 for /request-unlock; unknown→422 for /confirm-unlock
  4. rate-limiting     — shared unlk:ip: bucket (burst=3): 2×/request-unlock + 1×/confirm-unlock → 4th → 429

All test users use @e2e.test emails (or gmail_test_email) so `make _e2e-db-clean` can wipe them.
Run `make _e2e-db-clean` and `make _e2e-kv-clean` before this collection.

Notes on what is NOT covered here and why:
  - Admin-locked account (AdminLocked=true) → silent no-op: three distinct cases covered by service_test.go; HTTP surface is always 202.
  - Both admin-locked and time-locked simultaneously: same reasoning — service_test.go covers it; API surface is identical.
  - Token row created in account_unlock_tokens: covered by store_test.go TestRequestUnlockTx_Integration.
  - unlock_requested audit row written: covered by same store integration test.
  - Active token cooldown (no new OTP when one already exists): covered by service_test.go “active token exists — returns zero result nil error (cooldown)”.
  - Mail delivery failure → 202: covered by handler_test.go “non-empty RawCode with failing mailer still returns 202”.
  - Service-level error → 202: covered by handler_test.go “service error still returns 202 (anti-enumeration)”.
  - Wrong OTP reaching max-attempts → 429: covered by service_test.go + handler_test.go “ErrTooManyAttempts returns 429”.
  - Expired OTP → 422: requires time manipulation; covered by service_test.go “expired token returns ErrTokenExpired”.
  - Account becomes admin-locked after OTP issued → 423: regression-tested in handler_test.go; requires post-issuance state mutation not practical in E2E.
  - is_locked=false and failed_login_attempts=0 in DB after success: covered by store_test.go TestUnlockAccountTx_Integration (“clears lock”); indirectly confirmed by login-after-unlock step.
  - account_unlocked audit row written: covered by same store integration test.
  - Timing invariant (±100 ms): not reliably assertable in any CI environment; covered by unit-level “no panic” assertions on GetDummyOTPHash.

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// The rate-limiting folder overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 via its own prerequest.

item.name: happy-path
item.description:
Registers the gmail_test_email user, fetches the verification OTP from Gmail, verifies the email, triggers a time-lock via 10 consecutive wrong-password logins, calls POST /request-unlock and fetches the unlock OTP from Gmail.

Followed immediately by the confirm-unlock scenario sequence (order is critical):
  1. wrong OTP (1 attempt, below max-attempts) → 422
  2. correct OTP → 200
  3. login after unlock → 200 (indirectly confirms login_locked_until=NULL in DB)
  4. OTP replay (consumed code) → 422 (ErrTokenAlreadyUsed)
  5. no active token (account unlocked) → 422 (ErrTokenNotFound)

exec: // Use the real Gmail address from the environment so the verification email
// is delivered to a live inbox that gmail_access_token can read.
// The address is cleaned by _e2e-db-clean before each run.
console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
// Placed in the test script (not prerequest) because pm.sendRequest is async.
// The callback executes during the --delay-request idle window so
// unlk_vfy_code is set in time for the next request.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Verification OTP stored in unlk_vfy_code:', match[1]);
// Validate that the Gmail fetch in the register step completed successfully.
console.error('[ERROR] unlk_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
// The test script fires attempts #2–10 via chained pm.sendRequest calls,
// each on a distinct IP so the lgn:ip: rate limiter (burst=5) is never
// exhausted while the per-account failed_login_attempts counter climbs to 10.
// After 10 failures IncrementLoginFailures sets login_locked_until in the DB.
// Attempt #1 should return 401 invalid_credentials (account is not yet locked).
// Fire attempts #2–10 sequentially via chained pm.sendRequest callbacks.
// Each uses a distinct IP (10.20.0.N) to avoid exhausting the lgn:ip: bucket.
// The callbacks execute during the --delay-request window (>= 8000 ms recommended).
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ identifier: email, password: wrongPwd }) }
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] wrong-login #' + n + ' failed:', err); return; }
else console.log('[INFO] Lockout loop complete — account should now be time-locked.');

item.name: POST /request-unlock → 202 (issue unlock OTP, fetch from Gmail)

exec: // The lockout chain from the previous step's test script is async.
// Use --delay-request >= 8000 to ensure all 10 wrong logins complete
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and locked, an unlock code has been sent');
// Placed in the test script so the async callback has time to complete
// during the --delay-request window before the next request fires.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for unlock email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API for unlock OTP...');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching unlock email found in Gmail.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode unlock email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in unlock email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Unlock OTP stored in unlk_otp:', match[1]);

item.name: POST /confirm-unlock → 422 (wrong OTP, 1 attempt, below max-attempts threshold)

exec: // Validate that unlk_otp was fetched by the request-unlock step above.
console.error('[ERROR] unlk_otp is empty — Gmail fetch in request-unlock did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
// Submit a deliberately wrong 6-digit code (000000). One failed attempt
// is well below the max-attempts threshold so the token remains active
// for the correct-OTP step that follows.
// ErrInvalidCode is mapped to validation_error by the handler.
console.error('[ERROR] unlk_otp is empty — confirm-unlock will fail with a validation error.');

item.name: POST /login → 200 (account usable after unlock — indirectly confirms login_locked_until=NULL in DB)

exec: // A 200 from /login confirms that login_locked_until was cleared and
// failed_login_attempts was reset inside the UnlockAccountTx transaction.
// The token was consumed by the correct-OTP step above.
// Replaying the same code must return 422 validation_error, not 200.
// ErrTokenAlreadyUsed is mapped to validation_error (no hint about token existence).

item.name: POST /confirm-unlock → 422 (no active token after unlock — ErrTokenNotFound)

exec: // Account is unlocked, token is consumed. Any code for this email

item.name: validation
item.description: All input-validation cases for both POST /request-unlock and POST /confirm-unlock. Validation fires before any DB lookup so non-existent emails are safe to use throughout.

item.name: anti-enumeration
item.description:
Anti-enumeration contract:
  /request-unlock: every path returns 202 + identical body regardless of account state.
  /confirm-unlock: unknown email returns same 422 validation_error as the no-token case — no 404 ever returned.

exec: // Register a fresh @e2e.test account inline. It will be unverified, which
// causes the service to short-circuit at the email_verified guard — the same
// silent no-op that fires for a verified-but-not-locked account.
// The HTTP response is always 202 regardless of which suppression path fires.
body: { mode: 'raw', raw: JSON.stringify({ display_name: 'NotLocked', email: email, password: 'P@ssw0rd1!' }) }
console.warn('[WARN] Could not register unlk-notlocked account:', err || res.code);
pm.test('body is the correct anti-enumeration message', () => {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and locked, an unlock code has been sent');
// Ghost email: no account row exists. Service returns silent no-op 202.
pm.test('body is the correct anti-enumeration message', () => {
pm.expect(pm.response.json().message).to.eql('if that email is registered and locked, an unlock code has been sent');
// No account row for this email. ErrTokenNotFound is returned — the
// response is identical to the no-active-token case: no enumeration possible.

item.name: rate-limiting
item.description:
Verifies the shared unlk:ip: rate limiter (burst=3, rate=3/10 min) that covers both /request-unlock and /confirm-unlock.

The folder prerequest overrides _xff to 127.0.0.1 so all requests share one bucket.

Sequence:
  1. warmup 1/3 — POST /request-unlock  (slot 1)
  2. warmup 2/3 — POST /request-unlock  (slot 2)
  3. warmup 3/3 — POST /confirm-unlock  (slot 3, cross-endpoint consumption)
  4. 4th request — POST /request-unlock → 429 (bucket exhausted)

Covers both checklist items:
  • 4th request from same IP within 10 min → 429 (unlk:ip: limit)
  • 2× /request-unlock + 1× /confirm-unlock → 4th request (either endpoint) → 429

exec: // Override the collection-level unique IP so every request in this
// folder uses 127.0.0.1 as the client IP. All warmup requests share
// one bucket and exhaust it together.
// This request uses /confirm-unlock — a different endpoint from the
// previous two warmups. Because both endpoints share the unlk:ip: key,
// this cross-endpoint request still consumes a slot from the same bucket.
pm.test('not rate-limited yet (unlk:ip: slot 3, cross-endpoint confirm-unlock)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));
// Three slots consumed across both endpoints; the 4th request must be
// rate-limited regardless of which endpoint it targets.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter used by the collection prerequest to generate unique X-Forwarded-For IPs.
Current X-Forwarded-For value. Set to a unique IP by the collection prerequest; overridden to 127.0.0.1 by the rate-limiting folder prerequest.
Set by happy-path/register. The gmail_test_email address: verified + time-locked. Reused across all confirm-unlock scenario steps.
Set by happy-path/register. Used for the post-unlock login check.
Verification OTP fetched from Gmail by happy-path/register test script. Consumed by happy-path/verify-email.
Unlock OTP fetched from Gmail by happy-path/request-unlock test script. Consumed by happy-path/confirm-unlock (correct OTP step).
